creator: m.walraven@terantula.com
text:
On Tue, Apr 29, 2008 at 02:21:41PM +0200, Pav Lucistnik wrote:
> Did the index build actually failed? I do see "Done." printed.
> The line emitted about failure to launch python is just a warning.
> I do see it on pointyhat a lot.
Well it doesn't fail if you do a make index in /usr/ports. I do have a correct
INDEX in the end. So that's good. It seemed to fail cause our custom ports did
not show up in the ports.tgz during the make release process. However that was
a misconfiguration on our side :|.
 
> BTW it's a problem with the individual port. It really should be using
> python version as provided by bsd.python.mk instead of doing it's own
> calls.
I fully agree; maybe the mainainer can fix it so that it won't raise any
warnings instead.

Regards,

Marco
